troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.description: Common errors and how to fix them

categoryView.emptyDesc: Items will be filled in after general availability.

getting-started.q: How do I create a ClassAuto account?
getting-started.a: During the beta, sign up requires school-email verification. Submit the form at /beta-apply and you will receive a sign-up email within 1–2 business days.

getting-started.q: What should I do right after signing in for the first time?
getting-started.a: Follow the welcome modal's 5-step guide: department info → create a course → upload a PPT → review the AI script → share with students.

getting-started.q: Why does ClassAuto need my department info?
getting-started.a: It powers the dashboard's semester context (e.g., 'Week 8') and student notification copy. It is never exposed publicly and you can edit it any time.

getting-started.q: Can I explore a sample lecture first?
getting-started.a: Yes — visit /demo to experience the full learner-side flow in 5 minutes, no sign-up required.

video-creation.q: What PPT formats can I upload?
video-creation.a: We currently accept .pptx only. Including speaker notes dramatically improves the AI script quality.

video-creation.q: Can I publish AI-generated scripts as is?
video-creation.a: We recommend reviewing them before [Adopt]. Slides flagged ⚠ are ones the AI judged 'insufficient information' — those benefit most from manual edits.

video-creation.q: How long does it take to render one video?
video-creation.a: Usually 12–20 minutes depending on slide count and avatar queue. With background mode on we email you when it finishes.

video-creation.q: Can I render multiple videos at once?
video-creation.a: We currently recommend one render at a time per user. Concurrent queues can collide with cost guardrails — when broader concurrency ships, we'll announce it.

students.q: How do I share a lecture with students?
students.a: Once rendering finishes, the share panel offers URL, QR, and a short code. Sticking the QR on the last PPT slide or on the bulletin board makes attendance + access a single step.

students.q: Where can I see student progress?
students.a: /professor/dashboard shows the learner progress mix donut, and per-lecture analytics live at /professor/analytics/{lecture_id}.

students.q: Can I send a nudge to lagging students?
students.a: Use the Needs Attention widget's 'Lagging learners' section and click [Send nudge]. The button activates automatically once the notification endpoint ships.

students.q: Can I prevent students from watching without a password?
students.a: In the share panel you can enable 'school email only', 'password protect', and 'expire after a date' simultaneously.

billing.q: Is there any charge during the beta?
billing.a: Academic users pay nothing during the beta. There is a per-user monthly limit on video renders and Q&A calls (visible on the dashboard cost meter).

billing.q: What happens if I cross 80% of the limit?
billing.a: The cost meter pulses red as a warning. At 100%, new renders are blocked until you register payment or the limit resets next month.

billing.q: What happens if I upgrade to Pro mid-month?
billing.a: Limits expand immediately and Pro features (vulnerability heatmap, replay heatmap, etc.) activate. Downgrades take effect at the next billing cycle.
Yes, within 7 days of payment if usage stays below a small threshold. See the refund clause in /legal/terms for details.

security.a: No ads, automatic deletion after graduation, and per-school data isolation are the defaults. See /security and /trust for details.

security.q: Can anyone watch the lecture videos?
security.a: The default is 'school-email-verified students only'; you can additionally require a password, an expiration date, or an IP/domain restriction.

security.q: Can I export data to an external LMS?
security.a: You can export your own lecture data as CSV / SCORM at any time via the [Export CSV] button on the analytics page.

security.q: When I leave, is my data deleted right away?
security.a: After deletion, data stays recoverable for 60 days and is then permanently removed. For immediate erasure, email contact@classauto.live.

troubleshooting.a: 1) Confirm the file extension is .pptx 2) Confirm size < 100MB 3) Refresh the browser 4) If it still fails, attach the file in an email to contact@classauto.live.
Common on iOS Safari and some campus networks. Ask the student to try Chrome or switch from Wi-Fi to cellular.

troubleshooting.q: The AI script is too short.
troubleshooting.a: Usually means the speaker notes were sparse. Add 1–2 sentences per slide to the notes pane and re-upload.

troubleshooting.q: Q&A keeps rejecting my student's questions.
troubleshooting.a: Q&A only answers questions whose similarity to the lecture material is above 0.7. Out-of-scope questions are intentionally rejected — that is policy, not a bug.

cta.title: Didn't find what you need?
cta.description: We answer directly during the beta. For institutional adoption, contact us for a custom quote.
